Hi, I'm Rohan, a final year B.Tech (CSE) student from a Tier 2 college. I've always been fascinated by AI, and my goal is to become an AI developer. In college, we've covered the basics theoretically, but I feel completely lost when it comes to practical application. I struggle with knowing what specific skills companies are looking for, how to build a portfolio, or even how to start a real-world AI project. Most of my friends are already getting placed, and I'm really stressed about securing a good job after graduation. My family has high expectations, and I need to start earning soon.

I know I need to learn Python deeply, understand machine learning algorithms practically, and get hands-on with frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch. Right now, my confidence is low because I don't have the practical skills or projects to show. My dream is to land an entry-level AI developer role in a good tech company. The urgency is very high as placement season is already here. I can commit about 5 hours every week to dedicated learning, and I'm really hoping for clear guidance.
